title: New ESPN mock draft has Patriots choosing a ‘pass-rushing menace’ in first round
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN mock draft predicts Patriots will select Texas A&M edge rusher Cashius Howell with the No. 31 pick.
Howell recorded 11.5 sacks in 2025, ranking seventh among FBS defensive linemen, and led the SEC with 41 pressures.
Patriots likely seek to bolster pass rush, as current lineup ranked 27th in sacks during the regular season with 35.

title: Salem State student’s killer convicted 40 years after her murder, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, was convicted of first-degree murder for the 1986 death of Salem State student Claire Gravel.
Carey was indicted in 2022 after DNA evidence linked him to Gravel's murder 26 years after her death.
Carey faces life in prison without parole and will be sentenced on March 26, prosecutors confirmed.

title: N.H. woman accused of civil rights violation after allegedly shooting at lost man because he was Black
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Diane Durgin, 67, of Weare, N.H., accused of civil rights violations for allegedly shooting at a Black man on her property.
Durgin allegedly fired shots at X.G.'s car after he mistakenly drove to her home, mistaking him for a thief due to his race.
Prosecutors seek injunction to prevent Durgin from contacting the victim or repeating the alleged conduct, pending trial.

title: Holden rejects second MBTA Communities proposal, faces legal action
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Holden rejected a proposal to comply with the MBTA Communities Act, voting 520 to 257 against Article 2.
The proposal aimed to create multifamily overlay districts at Trap Rock quarry and Newbury Drive to meet state requirements.
Holden faces legal action from the Attorney General as a result of being noncompliant with the MBTA Communities Act.

title: Civil rights group wants AG to address LGBTQ-related bullying in school hockey
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lawyers for Civil Rights urges AG Andrea Campbell to tackle increased LGBTQ-related bullying in school hockey.
Reported incidents include homophobic slurs, harassment, and exclusion, with concerns linked to the 'Heated Rivalry' TV series.
The nonprofit sues MIAA for records, alleging failure to address and prevent identity-based harassment in sports.

title: Al Horford supports Luke Kornet’s stance on Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Al Horford supports Luke Kornet's letter urging the Atlanta Hawks to reconsider a promotion with strip club Magic City.
The promotion includes a performance by T.I., two wing flavors, and limited edition merchandise at a Hawks game.
Kornet expresses concern that the promotion contradicts the NBA's community image and standards for appropriate content.

title: Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs scrubs Instagram account
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stefon Diggs removed all posts from his Instagram page, keeping only temporary Instagram Story content.
Diggs's $6 million signing bonus becomes fully guaranteed on March 13 as he expects to return to the Patriots.
Diggs faces a pretrial hearing for assault and strangulation charges on April 1, related to an alleged December incident.

title: Get one-way flights for as low as $79 during JetBlue’s spring sale
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue offers one-way flights starting at $79 during its spring sale, ending March 5 at 11:59 p.m. EST.
The sale covers travel from April 14 to June 10, with blackout dates from May 21-26, on select flights.
Discounted flights include $79 to Daytona Beach, $119 to Los Angeles, and $229 to London from Logan Airport.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming in March
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Hulu adds Oscar nominees 'The Secret Agent', 'It Was Just an Accident', and 'Sentimental Value' in March 2026.
Netflix debuts 'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' movie continuation on March 20, 2026.
Steve Carell's new show 'Rooster' premieres on HBO Max on March 8, 2026.

title: Husband and wife charged with throwing rocks at passing cars in Southwick
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Celestine Robinson, 52, and David Ducharme, 42, charged with malicious damage and assault for rock-throwing incidents in Southwick.
Robinson also faces charges for drunk driving; her blood alcohol content registered at 0.127, surpassing the legal limit.
Ducharme held without bail after prosecutors highlighted past out-of-state charges including unspecified 'terroristic threats.'
